description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a rectangular array of solar photovoltaic panels mounted on a robust, ribbed base structure. The solar panels are arranged in a single, slightly elevated row, supported by a metallic frame that appears to be fixed and non-adjustable. The base is solid and likely constructed from metal or reinforced concrete, providing stability and protection for the electrical components beneath the panels. The surface of the panels is smooth and reflective, typical of tempered glass used in solar modules, while the base has a matte, industrial finish. No visible cabling or inverter housing is apparent from any angle, suggesting these components may be integrated within the base or otherwise concealed. The overall design is compact and utilitarian, suitable for placement in urban environments such as rooftops, parking lots, or dedicated solar parks.
description: A compact solar panel array mounted on a sturdy, ribbed base with a single row of photovoltaic panels and a metallic frame. The design is industrial and streamlined, with no visible cabling or inverter.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the solar panel array and the ribbed base structure beneath.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the base and the slight tilt of the solar panels.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the panel array and the solid, continuous base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the panel tilt and the ribbed texture of the base.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 20 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly discernible, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a ribbed metallic or concrete base and aluminum frame
attribute_array_configuration: single linear row of approximately 6 panels
